beats.full_text: Trying to outrun hesitation usually makes the loop more expensive. More rehearsing, more waiting for certainty. And less practice with the discomfort itself.
beats.ssot_text: Trying to outrun hesitation usually makes the loop more expensive: more rehearsing, more waiting for certainty, and less practice with the discomfort itself.

More rehearsing, more
beats.speakable_text: Trying to outrun hesitation usually makes the loop more expensive. More rehearsing, more waiting for certainty. And less practice with the discomfort itself.

beats.full_text: Hesitation often persists because the brain treats the discomfort of trying as more dangerous than the cost of staying still. Pushing harder can look like progress while the underlying pattern stays the same.
beats.ssot_text: Hesitation often persists because the brain treats the discomfort of trying as more dangerous than the cost of staying still. Pushing harder can look like progress while the underlying pattern stays the same.

as more dangerous than the
beats.speakable_text: Hesitation often persists because the brain treats the discomfort of trying as more dangerous than the cost of staying still. Pushing harder can look like progress while the underlying pattern stays the same.

beats.full_text: Try this. Name the smallest true next step, set a two-minute timer. And start before the fear finishes its case. Keep it small enough to finish before your brain starts negotiating.
beats.ssot_text: Try this: name the smallest true next step, set a two-minute timer, and start before the fear finishes its case. Keep it small enough to finish before your brain starts negotiating.

beats.full_text: Bookmark the pattern. Signal, reset, next action. You do not need to feel ready to be capable of the next step.
